fields.description:
## Context
rebar config is a BESPOKE flat key=value file (.rebar/config.conf / .rebar.conf / $REBAR_CONFIG) read by TWO independent ad-hoc inline "="-splitting parsers (_engine_support/lookups.py::_display_mode, _commands/txn.py verify-gate loop), plus ~40 distinct env vars across ~150 read sites with no single source of truth. A subagent researched what popular, actively-maintained Python CLI/dev-tools (ruff, black, mypy, pytest, pip, uv, poetry, coverage) converged on; a second subagent reviewed this epic. This aligns rebar with those conventions.

## Research-backed target (community convergence)
- FORMAT: TOML under [tool.rebar] in pyproject.toml + optional standalone rebar.toml, via stdlib tomllib (PEP 518/621/680). Replaces the bespoke flat parser (silent "="-in-value bugs, no types/nesting, silent unknown-key drop).
- DISCOVERY: walk UP from cwd to nearest project config (stop at rebar.toml / [tool.rebar] / .git / fs root); fall back to user-level XDG (~/.config/rebar/) only when no project config exists.
- PRECEDENCE (documented): CLI flag > REBAR_<KEY> env var > project config > user config > built-in defaults.
- SAFETY: single typed Config (source of truth); reject/warn on unknown keys (typo guard vs todays silent drop); fail-closed on parse error; alias old keys during a deprecation window; a rebar config/--show-config command for precedence transparency.
- SECRETS stay in env / gitignored .env (JIRA_API_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), NOT committed config.

## HARD CONSTRAINTS (rebar-specific; deviations from the research)
- Core is stdlib-only (pyproject dependencies = []): so NO pydantic-settings (use a stdlib dataclass Config) and NO platformdirs (hand-roll XDG via $XDG_CONFIG_HOME / ~/.config).
- tomllib is fine: runtime floor is ALREADY requires-python>=3.11 (the ruff target-version=py310 is only a LINT target, intentionally conservative) — NO tomli fallback / NO core dep needed.
- Config files are ordinary WORKING-TREE content on main, NOT events on the tickets branch — so there is NO event-log migration and config is not auto-pushed/merged by the store. Back-compat is plain file compat.
- Module-size policy: EXTEND the existing src/rebar/config.py (55 LOC) into the typed-Config home; do not spawn a sub-100-LOC fragment. Keep parity with / retire its bash mirror _engine/rebar-config.sh.
- Live config-file keys today are ONLY verify.require_signature_for_close (legacy alias verify.require_verdict_for_close) and ticket.display_mode (paths.*/planning.* were already removed).

## Success Criteria
- One typed Config source of truth; both inline parsers retired; no scattered ad-hoc config reads.
- TOML config + documented precedence; bespoke flat parser retired behind a back-compat alias window.
- Unknown-key + parse-error handling is loud, not silent — and the fail-closed verify gate is preserved.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ADR-style note (docs/) of format/precedence/discovery; confirm tomllib usable (floor already >=3.11, no fallback) — optionally bump ruff target py310->py311 as trivial cleanup
- [ ] Single typed Config (extend src/rebar/config.py, stdlib dataclass) as source of truth + validation + loud unknown-key handling
- [ ] TOML support ([tool.rebar] + rebar.toml) via tomllib, upward discovery + hand-rolled XDG user fallback (no platformdirs)
- [ ] Route ALL config-file reads through Config and RETIRE both inline parsers (lookups.py::_display_mode, txn.py verify loop)
- [ ] PRESERVE fail-closed verify gate: present-but-unreadable verify.* => require signature; absent => gate off — with a regression test
- [ ] Back-compat: old .rebar/config.conf reads IDENTICALLY for >=1 release; legacy keys aliased; unknown keys WARN (not fail) during deprecation, hard-error only after
- [ ] rebar config / --show-config: resolved values + which layer each came from
- [ ] Env-var standardization tracked as a SEPARATE linked child (REBAR_<KEY> prefixing is a breaking rename of established unprefixed vars PROJECT_ROOT/JIRA_*/TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR/... each needing an alias window) — informed by the interactive env-var review
- [ ] Docs (README/CLAUDE.md/docs) updated; full non-integration suite green
